FOnlineUserCacheAccelByte@cpp.name: FOnlineUserCacheAccelByte
FOnlineUserCacheAccelByte@cpp.description: Manages users that are queried from thebackend, making bulk calls to retrieve user data, as well as getting extra necessary information for those users, such as platform IDs relevant to the current native platform. As an explanation of how this works under the hood, the cache is made of sharedinstances that are put into two maps. One map is for mapping theID to the user's information, and one is for mapping platform information to the user's information. This way either can be queried seamlessly to get the same user data. The only time you won't be able to query a user by their platform IDs is if they are not on the same platform as you, in which you can only query byID. User data will be kept cached based on how long it has been since they have been accessed. You can configure how long users will stay in cache with thevariable in thesettings in. Users will also not be purged if they were marked as important when queried.

CacheLock.description: Mutex used to lock maps while we add to or retrieve from cache

UserCachePurgeTimeoutSeconds.description: Length of time in seconds that a user will stay in the cache without being accessed before being purged. Defaults to 600 seconds, or 10 minutes.

AccelByteIdToUserInfoMap.description: User cache that mapsIDs to shared user instances

PlatformIdToUserInfoMap.description: User cache that maps platform type and ID to shared user instances. The key is just a string that combines both type and ID, in the following format: "TYPE;ID".

Subsystem.description: online subsystem instance that owns this user cache.

Caches any results that we get from the backend, as well as will not query from the backend again if a duplicate is found.
